channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he won't 'fire people' over Signal messages, reiterates support of national security team
item.description: President Donald Trump is making his clearest commitment to not fire anyone over an embarrassing accidental leak of his administration’s plans for an airstrike against the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, have received email notices of their mass firing — the latest step in the Trump administration’s government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: High-profile ICE arrests of Tufts, Alabama students spotlight growing Trump sweep
item.description: Headline-grabbing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) arrests this week at Tufts University and the University of Alabama show growing momentum behind the Trump administration's battle against pro-Palestinian foreign students and faculty.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota food shelves worried as USDA cancels truckloads of food
item.description: This week, the U.S. Department of Agriculture canceled almost half of food shelf orders from Minnesota farmers under the federal Commodity Credit Corporation. Organizations providing nutrition programs say they are concerned about how to provide for their clientele.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Republican lawmaker confronted over Signal chat leak, responds sharply
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn., had a harsh response to a man asking him about his trust in the Trump team after the Signal chat leak. The congressman's reply seemed to stop the man in his tracks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey's GOP primary for governor could pivot on Trump, a part-time resident
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — The most important Republican in New Jersey's race for governor this year might well be a part-time resident of Bedminster who burnished his reputation and his brand near the Atlantic City Boardwalk.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump increasingly asks the Supreme Court to overrule judges blocking key parts of his agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As losses mount in lower federal courts, President Donald Trump has returned to a tactic that he employed at the Supreme Court with remarkable success in his first term.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No timetable from Texas governor on filling late Democratic congressman's vacant seat
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — The race to fill a Texas congressional seat has candidates but no election date more than three weeks after Rep. Sylvester Turner’s death left a vacancy in a stronghold for Democrats, who are eager to cut into Republicans’ narrow U.S. House majority.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Statute of limitations for pandemic unemployment fraud expires; bipartisan coalition seeks extension
item.description: House lawmakers are calling on their Senate colleagues to follow their lead and pass a bill extending the statute of limitations for prosecuting pandemic-era unemployment fraud.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Noted economist honored by Trump warns his 25% tariffs could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns in a new analysis that President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on auto imports could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle and says the proposed taxes could weaken the ability of U.S. automakers to compete with their foreign counterparts.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota doctor: Hospitals in ‘existential crisis’ and potential Medicaid cuts won’t help
item.description: Dr. Rahul Koranne, president and CEO of the Minnesota Hospital Association, joined Cathy Wurzer to talk about what recent job loss and federal volatility mean for the health care of all Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Foragers want a seat at the rulemaking table as DNR mulls mushroom, berry bag limits
item.description: The DNR is considering bag limits on how much food people can take from state-managed lands. That gallon limit is ringing alarm bells for foraging groups like the Minnesota Mycological Society.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court allows recently incarcerated to serve on juries
item.description: Minnesotans who are on probation or supervised release for felony convictions will soon be allowed to serve on juries, following a recent decision by the Minnesota Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Most Florida voters approve of President Trump, Gov. DeSantis in office, new poll shows
item.description: A new poll shows that the majority of Florida voters approve of the work being done by President Donald Trump and Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
